strengths: 6+ years of full-stack experience with TypeScript and Node.js across multiple Berlin companies
Hands-on exposure to AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling at current role at re:cinq
Track record of testing discipline (TDD, Selenium courses, custom test runner) aligning with Vitest/Jest requirements
concerns: No demonstrated Nest.js experience anywhere in the resume despite it being a primary stack requirement
React experience is thin and only briefly mentioned at Optilyz; most frontend work cites Ember.js or Dlang-based stacks
No Kotlin experience shown and Java exposure is limited to robotic-arm work nearly a decade ago

key_factors.factor: Shallow React track record vs. Ember-heavy frontend history
No Kotlin and dated Java exposure

response.justification: The candidate's long career leans heavily on Dlang, Ember.js, and assorted backend stacks rather than the specific Nest.js + React + Vitest toolchain this role centers on. React appears only fleetingly at Optilyz, Nest.js is absent entirely, and the secondary Kotlin/Java requirement is unmet beyond a decade-old robotic-arm project. While AI-agent work at re:cinq is relevant, much of the depth shown (Dlang, GIS, embedded, legacy PHP CMS) does not transfer to the stated stack, making this a weak alignment with the role's core technical needs.
